up.method: HPLC (Biorad Aminex HPX-87H, RI detector); consumption tracked over time

sec.units: % of maximum theoretical yield (77% at 30 h during sampling; >95% at 48 h final)
co-product (ethanol/acetate ratio not stated for glucose)

growth_records.cite: Tolonen AC et al. Proteome-wide systems analysis of a cellulosic biofuel-producing microbe. Molecular Systems Biology (2011). DOI:10.1038/msb.2010.116. [PMC3049413]
growth_records.snippet: Ethanol titers reached 77% of the maximum theoretical yield in the glucose cultures (30 h, Figure 2D)... Final ethanol concentrations in glucose cultures were >95% of the maximum theoretical yield after 48 h.
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic growth of C. phytofermentans on 3 g/L glucose in GS2 medium at 32C. No specific growth rate (mu) or doubling time reported numerically in main text (growth curves in Figure 2A; quantitative values in Supplementary Figs S2-5). No uptake/secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h; only ethanol product yields as % of maximum theoretical (77% at 30 h sampling, >95% final after 48 h). GS2 medium full composition not stated (cited Johnson et al 1981). Carbon source 3 g/L glucose is stated. pH not stated. Growth quantified as OD600 (OD600=1.1 ~ 1e9 cells/mL). Fermentation products by HPLC Aminex HPX-87H.

up.method: HPLC / OD600; growth faster on hemicellulose than glucose or xylose

sec.units: % of maximum theoretical yield (27% at 24 h during sampling)
elevated relative to cellulose (higher acetate:ethanol than cellulose)

growth_records.cite: Tolonen AC et al. Proteome-wide systems analysis of a cellulosic biofuel-producing microbe. Molecular Systems Biology (2011). DOI:10.1038/msb.2010.116. [PMC3049413]
growth_records.snippet: Growth was faster on hemicellulose (Figure 2B) than on glucose (Figure 2A) or xylose... Ethanol titers reached ...27% in the hemicellulose cultures (24 h, Figure 2E) during the sampling period.
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic growth on 3 g/L birch wood hemicellulose xylan in GS2 at 32C. Growth reported qualitatively faster than on glucose/xylose but no numeric mu or doubling time in main text. Ethanol yield 27% of maximum theoretical at 24 h sampling; more acetate produced relative to ethanol than on cellulose (acetate accumulation partly from acetyl side chains of hardwood xylan). No mmol/gDW/h rates. Medium (GS2) full composition not stated; only carbon source stated. pH not stated.

up.units: g/L (initial 10); linear degradation rate (dry mass remaining measured by filtration/drying/weighing)
up.method: Gravimetric (dry mass of cellulose remaining, 11 um filter, dried 65C)

sec.units: % of maximum theoretical yield (68% direct conversion of cellulose to ethanol; linear formation)

growth_records.cite: Tolonen AC et al. Proteome-wide systems analysis of a cellulosic biofuel-producing microbe. Molecular Systems Biology (2011). DOI:10.1038/msb.2010.116. [PMC3049413]
growth_records.snippet: Stable cell densities (10^7-10^8 CFU ml-1) in the cellulose cultures resulted in linear rates of cellulose degradation and ethanol formation that correspond to a direct conversion of cellulose to ethanol at 68% of the maximum theoretical yi
growth_records.notes: Batch anaerobic growth on 10 g/L cellulose (Whatman #1 filter paper) in GS2 at 32C, sampled after 2 weeks. Cell density stable at 1e7-1e8 CFU/mL (non-exponential; linear cellulose degradation and ethanol formation) so no mu / doubling time defined. Ethanol = 68% of max theoretical yield; ethanol:acetate = 9.54 (highest reported for clostridia). No mmol/gDW/h uptake/secretion rates; cellulose consumption measured gravimetrically. GS2 full composition not stated; only carbon source (10 g/L cellulose) stated. pH not stated.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 2841 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/2841
